behavior: Selects first ACTIVE membership with take:1 and NO orderBy — DB-dependent non-deterministic among ACTIVE memberships. INVITED/REMOVED/SUSPENDED memberships excluded by where status=ACTIVE. Does not check Organization.status separately.
codeEvidence: AuthController.login include memberships where ACTIVE take:1

behavior: Re-selects newest ACTIVE membership orderBy createdAt desc take:1. Ignores which org was in previous access token.
canCrossOrgIssueAccessToken: YES if user has multiple ACTIVE memberships

behavior: Same as login: ACTIVE take:1 no orderBy.
Prefer JWT organizationId if ACTIVE membership exists; else oldest ACTIVE membership orderBy createdAt asc.
YES — refresh can change organizationId claim if newest ACTIVE membership differs from login selection or membership set changes.

NO — login/refresh/me filter status ACTIVE only so REMOVED not chosen. Caveat: org suspend leaves membership ACTIVE and sets User.status SUSPENDED — refresh then fails on user.status check not membership.
codeEvidence: where status ACTIVE; rotate user.status!==ACTIVE throws

behavior: INVITED memberships not selected (filter ACTIVE). User with only INVITED cannot get org claim until accept/create activates membership.
NO — RefreshToken columns: userId tokenHash family expiresAt revokedAt replacedBy userAgent ipAddress. No organizationId membershipId roleVersion.
NO — family is UUID string only; no org/membership state on family.
Bound to userId (+ family for rotation). NOT bound to organizationId or membershipId. Access JWT carries org snapshot until expiry.

behavior: N/A — no switch. Login always creates new family via issueTokenPair→createRefreshToken new UUID. Refresh keeps same family.
YES — opaque refresh is user-global; rotate loads newest ACTIVE membership and signs JWT for that org even if prior access was Org A.

preliminaryRisk: P1 — H2 access token after removal
